title: paeonia peregrina proles officinalis
intro: Paeonia peregrina proles officinalis, also known as Paeonia lactiflora or Garden Peony, is a perennial plant native to Asia and Europe, cultivated for its ornamental value and medicinal properties. The roots and flowers of this plant contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Paeonia peregrina proles officinalis include its ability to soothe menstrual cramps, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, while also providing relief from digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being and balance.
